DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-02-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Cross-Defendants Katerina Stolyarov and Leonid Stolyarov's demurrer to the third cause of action for fraud and deceit in the first amended cross-complaint is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. Cross-Complainants Joseph and Maria Kristul fail to allege that the representations made by the Stolyarovs were false and that the Stolyarovs knew the representations were false or made them recklessly and without regard for their truth. Because it is reasonably possible that the Kristuls can correct these deficiencies, they are given twenty days leave to amend if they can do so in good faith. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-07-2016 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendants Joseph Kristul and Maria Kristul's demurrer to all four causes of action in the complaint filed by plaintiffs Katerina and Leonid Stolyarov is overruled as to the first cause of action for breach of contract, the second cause of action for accounting, and the third cause of action for financial elder abuse and sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action for constructive fraud. The complaint adequately alleges the first three claims. None of those claims are uncertain or unintelligible. Nor does the complaint necessarily disclose on its face that the first cause of action is time-barred. The third cause of action is sufficient to state a viable claim per the Elder Abuse Act. The fourth cause of action fails to allege the required element of a confidential or fiduciary relationship. Plaintiffs are granted leave to correct this deficiency if they can do so in good faith. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/HK)
